scope: 1100-1788 England; unresolved attestations and out-of-scope records only

leads.label: Expanded target surname forms systematic scan
leads.summary: The active target list now includes Conigrave, Conygrave, Coneygrave, Conygreve, Congrave, Congreve, Congreave, Cunygrave, Cungrave, Cunegrave, Comegrave, and Cumgrave.
leads.nextAction: Search parish, probate, court, tax, guild, manor, and manuscript indexes for each target spelling, then promote only source-backed English birth, lived, or death evidence.

leads.label: Congreve of Congreve and Stretton lineage
leads.summary: Published county-gentry material says the family was seated at Congreve soon after the Conquest and that William Congreve removed to Stretton in the reign of Edward II after marrying the Campion heiress. This is lineage context, not a vital event.
leads.nextAction: Trace medieval deeds, inquisitions, heralds' visitations, and Penkridge/Stretton parish evidence for individual births, deaths, or burials.

leads.label: Thomas Conygrave, Windsor clockmaker attestation
leads.summary: St George's Chapel notes a payment to Thomas Conygrave for a clock in 1490. This is a strong occupational/location lead but not a birth or death event.
leads.nextAction: Search Windsor, Berkshire, and royal household records for baptism, will, burial, or family links.

leads.label: William Connigrave/Conygrave, London attestation cluster
leads.summary: The London Subsidy Roll normalized index groups William under Connigrave with spellings Conigraue, Conigrave, Connygrave, and Conygrave. The printed index to Returns of Aliens also indexes William as Connigrave/Conygrave. These are strong identity/spelling leads but not vital events.
leads.nextAction: Inspect the subsidy membranes and alien-return volume pages, then search London parish, freedom, denization, will, and burial records.

leads.label: Reginald/Reynold and Joan Conygrave, Kensworth manor lead
leads.summary: Bedfordshire Archives and Historic England record Reynold/Reginald Conygrave and wife Joan in a Kensworth/Dunstable manor transaction. The public pages disagree on 1534 versus 1544, so the original archive item needs inspection.
leads.nextAction: Inspect Bedfordshire Archives F130 and related feet of fines; then search London merchant tailor, probate, and burial records for Reginald/Reynold and Joan.

leads.label: Reginald/Reynold Conygrave, Common Pleas litigation
leads.summary: AALT Common Pleas indexes list Reginald/Reynold Conygrave of London, merchant tailor, in debt and indenture actions. The occupational identifier probably matches the Kensworth manor actor, but no birth/death event is present.
leads.nextAction: Resolve CP40 roll dates and parties, then link to Merchant Taylors' Company records, London parish registers, and PCC wills.

leads.label: Thomas Congreve of Stretton household manuscript
leads.summary: Cambridge cites Thomas Congreve's Staffordshire Record Office commonplace book and diary. It identifies Thomas as a Staffordshire gentleman and landowner, with wife Margaret, daughters Isobel and Elizabeth, eldest son Frances/Francis and daughter-in-law Dorothy active in family birth events.
leads.nextAction: Inspect Staffordshire Record Office MS D1057/0/1 for exact dates and then search Penkridge/Stretton baptisms and burials for each named household member.

leads.label: FreeREG Ecton CONIGRAVE register occurrence
leads.summary: FreeREG's St Mary Magdalene, Ecton, Northamptonshire register-name page includes CONIGRAVE among surnames, but follow-up FreeREG searching did not expose a usable exact event row. Treat FreeREG as exhausted for this exact Conigrave lead.
leads.nextAction: Do not repeat broad FreeREG searches for this lead. Check the Ecton parish register image, Northamptonshire transcript, or other non-FreeREG parish material directly.

leads.label: FreeREG Tottenham CONGREVE/CONIGRAVE register occurrence
leads.summary: FreeREG's Phillimore transcript for All Hallows, Tottenham, lists both CONGREVE and CONIGRAVE as surnames, but follow-up FreeREG searching did not yield additional usable exact Conigrave event rows. The parish remains a source lead; FreeREG itself is exhausted for the Conigrave spelling.
leads.nextAction: Search All Hallows Tottenham parish material outside FreeREG for both spellings, then separate baptisms, burials, marriages, witnesses, and parent mentions.

leads.label: Coventry exact Conigrave parish-register follow-up
leads.place: St Michael and Holy Trinity, Coventry, Warwickshire
leads.summary: The promoted St Michael monument cluster gives deaths for Mary, Benjamin, and Ann Conigrave from 1754 to 1787. Coventry Archives catalogue evidence now adds Benjamin's 1754 charity-loan security and 1769 will, naming sons William and Joseph, but no baptism, marriage, or burial-register image has been extracted.
leads.nextAction: Search Coventry St Michael and Holy Trinity registers, wills, apprenticeship records, and local indexes for exact Conigrave baptisms, marriages, burials, and children linked to Benjamin, Mary, Ann, William, Alice, or Joseph.

leads.label: Coventry Conigrave grandchildren in 1799 partition
leads.summary: A 1799 Coventry Archives lease and release says James Conigrave, Mary Francis, Ann Wesson, and Elizabeth Conigrave were adult children of William Conigrave of Coventry, carpenter, deceased son of the late Benjamin Conigrave of Coventry. The document is post-1788, so these grandchildren still need in-scope baptisms, marriages, burials, apprenticeship records, or earlier property evidence before display.
leads.nextAction: Search Coventry St Michael, St John Baptist, Holy Trinity, Spon Street, Greyfriars Lane, and Foleshill records for in-scope exact Conigrave events naming James, Sarah, Mary, William Francis, Ann, Daniel Wesson, or Elizabeth.

leads.label: Conygrave spelling family
leads.summary: Variant regularisation sources connect older spellings such as Conygraue and Conigrave-style forms.
leads.nextAction: Include these spellings when searching manuscript catalogues, parish transcripts, and OCR corpora.

leads.label: FreeREG Conygrave scan
leads.summary: FreeREG should be searched systematically for CONYGRAVE and neighbouring spellings, then checked against parish images where possible.
leads.nextAction: Export candidate baptisms, burials, and marriages with parish, county, date, and register reference.

leads.label: John Congreave, Bridgnorth correspondence
leads.summary: Royal Society correspondence records John Congreave at Bridgnorth. This is useful spelling/location evidence inside the expanded date window, but it is not part of the exact-Conigrave pass.
leads.nextAction: Promote only during a Congreave variant pass after checking whether the correspondence supplies enough dated English lived evidence and family metadata.

leads.label: Elizabeth Congreave, Tatenhill marriage lead
leads.summary: A family genealogy page lists Elizabeth Congreave marrying Benjamin Brough in 1733. The event is inside the expanded date window but still needs parish-register verification before display.
leads.nextAction: Verify against Tatenhill parish registers and extract a source-backed marriage, baptism, burial, or lived event before considering display.

leads.label: Charles Walter Congreve, expanded-scope follow-up
leads.summary: Public biography gives birth and death years, and the 1704 birth now falls inside the expanded display window. This is a Congreve variant lead, not part of the exact-Conigrave pass.
leads.nextAction: Reassess during a Congreve variant pass; verify English birth/lived coordinates and family metadata before promotion.

leads.label: Ralph Congreve MP, expanded-scope follow-up
leads.summary: Public biography identifies Ralph Congreve as a London-associated MP, and the estimated birth now falls inside the expanded date window. This is a Congreve variant lead, not part of the exact-Conigrave pass.
leads.nextAction: Reassess during a Congreve variant pass; verify an English birth or dated lived event before promotion.

leads.label: George Congreve, non-English birth exclusion
leads.place: Born outside England; death after visible timeline
leads.summary: Relevant surname record but excluded from the map because the birth is not English and the dates are outside the 1100-1788 scope.
leads.nextAction: Keep as an exclusion note unless an English birth/death event within scope is found.

leads.label: Sir William Congreve, 1st Baronet, expanded-scope follow-up
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope; the Stafford birth now falls inside the expanded 1100-1788 range. This is a Congreve variant lead, not part of the exact-Conigrave pass.
leads.nextAction: Reassess during a Congreve variant pass; verify English birth/lived coordinates and family metadata before promotion.

leads.label: Sir William Congreve, 2nd Baronet, expanded-scope follow-up
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope; the London birth now falls inside the expanded 1100-1788 range. This is a Congreve variant lead, not part of the exact-Conigrave pass.
leads.nextAction: Reassess during a Congreve variant pass; keep the foreign death unplotted if promoted.

leads.label: Richard Congreve, post-1788 exclusion
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but both vital events are outside the expanded 1100-1788 range.
leads.nextAction: Retain only as later-family context.

leads.label: Sir Walter Norris Congreve, post-1788 exclusion
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but the birth is outside the narrowed range.
leads.nextAction: Retain as later-family context only.

leads.label: Cecil Ralph Townshend Congreve, post-1788 exclusion
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but the birth is outside the narrowed range.
leads.nextAction: Retain as later-family context only.

leads.label: William La Touche Congreve, post-1788 exclusion
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but the birth is outside the narrowed range.
leads.nextAction: Retain as later-family context only.

leads.label: Geoffrey Cecil Congreve, post-1788 exclusion
leads.summary: Previously display-ready under the 1100-1900 scope, but the birth is outside the narrowed range.
leads.nextAction: Retain as later-family context only.

leads.label: Charles Price Conigrave, non-English exclusion
leads.summary: Important Conigrave surname example but outside the English-locale scope.
leads.nextAction: Use only for spelling lineage research, not the England animation.

leads.label: Post-1788 London exact Conigrave marriage cluster
leads.place: Tottenham, Middlesex; City of London; Camberwell, Surrey; South Australia
leads.summary: Exact Conigrave marriage transcripts record James Conigrave at All Hallows, Tottenham in 1827, Benjamin Conigrave of St Botolph Aldersgate marrying Matilda Reeve at St Martin Vintry in 1832, and Joseph Conigrave of St Botolph Aldersgate marrying Amelia Boot at Camberwell St Giles in 1833. The Java passenger-list material then places Benjamin, Matilda, and children emigrating to South Australia in 1839/1840.
leads.nextAction: Use this cluster only as a lineage trail: search St Botolph Aldersgate, Tottenham, Camberwell, and nearby London registers for earlier exact Conigrave baptisms or burials that fall inside the 1100-1788 scope.

leads.label: Thomas Congreve D1057 commonplace book full extraction
leads.summary: TNA D1057 says Thomas Congreve's commonplace book records births, marriages, and deaths of numerous children and relatives. Public snippets support the household cluster, but the full manuscript is needed to replace broad pedigree placements with event-level records.
leads.nextAction: Obtain Staffordshire Record Office MS D1057/0/1 or a transcript and extract every named birth, marriage, death, kinship, and residence entry.

leads.label: Common Pleas Congreve/Congrave systematic scan
leads.summary: AALT indexes exposed Richard Congreve in 1440, Richard Congrave of Loughton in 1483, Margery and John Congreve in a 1521 Staffordshire dower action, and Ralph Congreve in a 1530 Staffordshire debt action.
leads.nextAction: Search all AALT CP40 plaintiff/defendant indexes for every active target spelling plus Connigrave and common OCR variants, then inspect membranes where family relationships may be stated.

leads.label: Surname dictionary original-record verification
leads.summary: SurnameDB and Forebears/Bardsley expose additional display-ready people and spouse households from rolls, Feet of Fines, parish marriages, Oxford register evidence, and a Canterbury marriage allegation.
leads.nextAction: Verify each surname-dictionary extraction against the original roll, parish register, university register, or Canterbury marriage allegation, then search each marriage household depth-first for baptisms, burials, fathers, and named children.
